user_prompt: Make a `lib\WifiManager\AsyncFsManager.cpp` and its associated header file
- init, called from any activation of Wi-Fi
- reset walk, reset iterator for file listing, should always work no matter what state
- walk one, return one file from directory, the next call should return the next file, return something to indicate end of list when needed, automatically restart the walk if something is wrong
- open file for download, closes any other file or anything else that might conflict, reset seek pointer
critically, all of these need to be thread safe
web server code can only use methods within this manager, add thread safe functions to this manager as needed
use it to attempt to fix the problem with listing files and the decrypted download
agent_reply: Implemented `AsyncFsManager` and routed the web SD access through it.
What changed:
- Added [AsyncFsManager.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.h:19) and [AsyncFsManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp:93).
- It owns the active walk handle and active download handle behind a recursive FreeRTOS mutex.
- `resetWalk`, `walkOne`, `closeWalk`, `openFileForDownload`, `readFileChunk`, and `closeFile` are all serialized.
- Opening a download closes any active walk; resetting a walk closes any active download.
- Added thread-safe helpers for web upload/delete/readiness: `writeFileChunk`, `removeFile`, `isFile`, `isReady`.
I also wired:
- `/list_files.json` to use `AsyncFsManager::resetWalk()` and `walkOne()` instead of holding its own `FsFile`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:221)
- `/download_file` to use `openFileForDownload()` / `readFileChunk()`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:413)
- decrypted download to use the manager for encrypted recording reads: [DecryptedDownload.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/DecryptedDownload.cpp:505)
- Wi-Fi activation reset via `AsyncFsManager::init()`: [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:397)
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passes.

user_prompt: the shared resource is SPI, I bet it's being interrupted by a GUI redraw
so thread safety isn't just about file system objects, it needs to work well with `FlyGuiView`'s polling calls
agent_reply: Agreed, and I implemented the first blunt-but-useful version of that coordination.
What changed:
- `AsyncFsManager::guiShouldYield()` now reports when a file walk or download is active.
- `FlyGui::poll()` now returns immediately while the async FS manager is active, so GUI redraw/touch polling won’t compete with SD reads during web listing/download.
- The reopen-and-retry SD read path I added before the interruption is still in place, with SD error code/data logging.
Build passes: `pio run -e thefly_sec1`.

This is intentionally conservative: during a browser file download, the on-device GUI may feel frozen until the transfer ends or disconnects. If this fixes the download corruption, we can refine it into a real shared SPI arbiter or timed “download quiet mode” instead of a broad GUI yield.

commit_message: file download and decrypted downloads work now

user_prompt: I got one successful upload, and a few that are partial, ending in a error 500, the file exists on the card but much smaller than expected
agent_reply: I changed the upload path to behave like a real upload session instead of reopening/appending/syncing the SD file on every network chunk.
What changed:

- `AsyncFsManager` now has `openFileForUpload()`, `writeUploadFileChunk()`, `closeUploadFile()`, and upload session IDs in [AsyncFsManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp).

- `/file_upload` now opens once on the first chunk, writes chunks sequentially by offset, verifies the final raw-body byte count, then syncs/closes once at finish in [WebFileHandlers.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp).
- Client disconnect cleanup now only closes the matching upload session, so a late disconnect cannot kill a newer upload.
- Web UI upload timeout went from 2 minutes to 10 minutes in [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js).
- `inc/web_assets.h` was regenerated by the build.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: success.
This should fix the “partial file then 500” behavior if it was caused by per-chunk SD sync/open/close overhead or browser timeout.

user_prompt: the first file, 5MB, was successful, the second and third both failed and not even created on the card, the dialog says "File write failed" but interestingly the progress bar is 100%
agent_reply: Yep, that 100% progress bar is only “browser finished sending bytes to the ESP,” not “ESP finished committing to SD.” The old message hid whether the failure was open/write/close.
I tightened this up:
- Raw uploads now preallocate the file when `Content-Length` is known.
- Uploads sync every 512 KB instead of relying on one big final flush.
- Final close now truncates preallocated size to actual bytes and checks `close()`.
- `removeFile()` no longer blindly closes whatever upload is active.
- UI/server errors are now more specific: `File open failed`, `File chunk write failed`, `File close failed`, or `Incomplete file upload`.
- `inc/web_assets.h` regenerated from the updated web JS.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: success.
If it still fails, the new dialog text plus serial log SD error codes should tell us exactly which phase is breaking.

user_prompt: hmmm I am trying to upload a file and I got `W (45542) AsyncFsManager: write file failed: path=/firmware.bin wanted=1436 wrote=0 sync_or_write_failed` and then `W (52681) AsyncFsManager: remove file failed: fs remove failed path=/firmware.bin`
I thought we recently fixed all the upload bugs
agent_reply: Yeah, this was a different upload bug class. The earlier fix covered web file operations colliding with each other; this one was the GUI still being allowed to draw on the shared SPI bus between HTTP upload chunks.
I changed:
- [AsyncFsManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp): tracks an active web upload and makes `guiShouldYield()` return true during the whole upload.
- [WebFileHandlers.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp): marks upload active on first chunk, clears it on success/error/disconnect.
- Added SdFat `sd_error` / `sd_data` to write/remove failure logs, so if it still fails we’ll get the real low-level clue.
The `remove file failed` was probably cleanup fallout after the write failed, not the primary failure.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passed.
- Plain `pio run` still fails because your current `inc/conf.h` requires `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` and the default env doesn’t define it.

user_prompt: Can you also make sure that the GUI isn't in an update? in `void FlyGui::poll()`, right before the call to `M5.update();`, mark the GUI as being busy, up to the end of the redraw call. The stuff in AsyncFsManager can wait on it internally. There isn't much that can happen in the GUI when Wi-Fi is on anyways.
agent_reply: Done. I bracketed `FlyGui::poll()` from just before `M5.update()` through `redraw(false)` with `AsyncFsManager::beginGuiUpdate()` / `endGuiUpdate()` in [FlyGui.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:143).

`AsyncFsManager` now tracks GUI-update ownership and makes filesystem operations wait while another task owns the GUI update. It skips the wait for the GUI task itself, so touch/button callbacks won’t deadlock if they indirectly touch SD.
Verified:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passed
No build processes left running.

user_prompt: update `AsyncFsManager::walkOne` to also return a file size
update `FileListJsonStream` to make each file represented by an actual object instead of just a string, the object can contain the file size
update `html\script.js` to handle the new data key in the JSON
update `html\script.js` and `html\index.html` so that the rendered table displays the file size beside the file name in brackets
agent_reply: Done. The file list API now returns objects like:
```json
[{ "name": "firmware.bin", "size": 123456 }]
Changes made:
- `AsyncFsManager::walkOne(...)` can now return file size via an optional `uint64_t*`.
- `FileListJsonStream` emits `{name, size}` objects instead of plain strings.
- `html/script.js` reads the new `size` key, formats it with `format_bytes()`, and still tolerates the old string-only list shape.
- `html/index.html` row templates now render `filename [size]`.
- `inc/web_assets.h` was regenerated by the PlatformIO build because the HTML/JS changed.
Verified:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passed

commit_message: web file list exposes file size
